packet.c(3) Library Functions Manual packet.c(3)

NAME

packet.c

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t packet_send (socket_t sockfd, packet_type_t type, const void *data, size_t len)
Send a packet with proper header and CRC32. asciichat_error_t packet_receive (socket_t sockfd, packet_type_t *type, void **data, size_t *len)
Receive a packet with proper header validation and CRC32 checking. asciichat_error_t send_packet_secure (socket_t sockfd, packet_type_t type, const void *data, size_t len, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Send a packet with encryption and compression support. packet_recv_result_t receive_packet_secure (socket_t sockfd, void *crypto_ctx, bool enforce_encryption, packet_envelope_t *envelope)
Receive a packet with decryption and decompression support. int send_packet (socket_t sockfd, packet_type_t type, const void *data, size_t len)
Send a basic packet without encryption. int receive_packet (socket_t sockfd, packet_type_t *type, void **data, size_t *len)
Receive a basic packet without encryption. int send_ping_packet (socket_t sockfd)
Send a ping packet. int send_pong_packet (socket_t sockfd)
Send a pong packet. asciichat_error_t packet_decrypt_envelope (packet_envelope_t *envelope, void *crypto_ctx)
Send a clear console packet. asciichat_error_t packet_send_error (socket_t sockfd, const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx, asciichat_error_t error_code, const char *message)
Send an error packet with optional encryption context. asciichat_error_t packet_parse_error_message (const void *data, size_t len, asciichat_error_t *out_error_code, char *message_buffer, size_t message_buffer_size, size_t *out_message_length)
Parse an error packet payload into components. asciichat_error_t packet_send_remote_log (socket_t sockfd, const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx, log_level_t level, remote_log_direction_t direction, uint16_t flags, const char *message)
Send a remote log packet with optional encryption context. asciichat_error_t packet_parse_remote_log (const void *data, size_t len, log_level_t *out_level, remote_log_direction_t *out_direction, uint16_t *out_flags, char *message_buffer, size_t message_buffer_size, size_t *out_message_length)
Parse a remote log packet payload into components. int send_protocol_version_packet (socket_t sockfd, const protocol_version_packet_t *version)
Send protocol version packet. int send_crypto_capabilities_packet (socket_t sockfd, const crypto_capabilities_packet_t *caps)
Send crypto capabilities packet. int send_crypto_parameters_packet (socket_t sockfd, const crypto_parameters_packet_t *params)
Send crypto parameters packet. asciichat_error_t av_send_audio_opus_batch (socket_t sockfd, const uint8_t *opus_data, size_t opus_size, const uint16_t *frame_sizes, int sample_rate, int frame_duration, int frame_count, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Send Opus-encoded audio batch packet with encryption support. asciichat_error_t send_image_frame_packet (socket_t sockfd, const void *image_data, uint16_t width, uint16_t height, uint8_t format)
Send image frame packet.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t packet_parse_error_message (const void * data, size_t len, asciichat_error_t * out_error_code, char * message_buffer, size_t message_buffer_size, size_t * out_message_length)

Parse an error packet payload into components.

Parameters

data Packet payload buffer
len Payload length in bytes
out_error_code Output pointer for asciichat_error_t value (must not be NULL)
message_buffer Destination buffer for message string (must not be NULL)
message_buffer_size Size of destination buffer in bytes (must be > 0)
out_message_length Optional output for message length reported by sender

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure

Definition at line 945 of file packet.c.

947                                                                          {
948   if (!data || len < sizeof(error_packet_t) || !out_error_code || !message_buffer || message_buffer_size == 0) {
949     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
950                      "Invalid parameters: data=%p len=%zu out_error_code=%p message_buffer=%p buffer_size=%zu", data,
951                      len, out_error_code, message_buffer, message_buffer_size);
952   }
953 
954   const error_packet_t *packet = (const error_packet_t *)data;
955   uint32_t raw_error_code = NET_TO_HOST_U32(packet->error_code);
956   uint32_t raw_message_length = NET_TO_HOST_U32(packet->message_length);
957 
958   if (raw_message_length > MAX_ERROR_MESSAGE_LENGTH) {
959     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Error message length too large: %u", raw_message_length);
960   }
961 
962   size_t total_required = sizeof(error_packet_t) + (size_t)raw_message_length;
963   if (total_required > len) {
964     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Error packet truncated: expected %zu bytes, have %zu", total_required,
965                      len);
966   }
967 
968   const uint8_t *message_bytes = (const uint8_t *)data + sizeof(error_packet_t);
969   size_t copy_len = raw_message_length;
970   if (copy_len >= message_buffer_size) {
971     copy_len = message_buffer_size - 1;
972   }
973 
974   if (copy_len > 0) {
975     memcpy(message_buffer, message_bytes, copy_len);
976   }
977   message_buffer[copy_len] = '\0';
978 
979   if (out_message_length) {
980     *out_message_length = raw_message_length;
981   }
982 
983   *out_error_code = (asciichat_error_t)raw_error_code;
984   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
985 }




References ASCIICHAT_OK, error_packet_t::error_code,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
    MAX_ERROR_MESSAGE_LENGTH, error_packet_t::message_length,
    NET_TO_HOST_U32, and SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t packet_parse_remote_log (const void *
  data, size_t len, log_level_t * out_level,
  remote_log_direction_t * out_direction, uint16_t * out_flags,
  char * message_buffer, size_t message_buffer_size, size_t *
  out_message_length)


Parse a remote log packet payload into components.


Parameters


data Packet payload buffer


len Payload length in bytes


out_level Output pointer for log level (must not be NULL)


out_direction Output pointer for direction (must not be NULL)


out_flags Output pointer for flags (must not be NULL)


message_buffer Destination buffer for message string (must not be NULL)


message_buffer_size Size of destination buffer in bytes (must be > 0)


out_message_length Optional output for message length reported by
  sender


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 1044 of file packet.c.


1047                                                                       {
1048   if (!data || len < sizeof(remote_log_packet_t) || !out_level || !out_direction || !out_flags || !message_buffer ||
1049       message_buffer_size == 0) {
1050     return SET_ERRNO(
1051         ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
1052         "Invalid parameters: data=%p len=%zu out_level=%p out_direction=%p out_flags=%p buffer=%p size=%zu", data, len,
1053         out_level, out_direction, out_flags, message_buffer, message_buffer_size);
1054   }
1055 
1056   const remote_log_packet_t *packet = (const remote_log_packet_t *)data;
1057   uint8_t raw_level = packet->log_level;
1058   if (raw_level > LOG_FATAL) {
1059     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid remote log level: %u", raw_level);
1060   }
1061   *out_level = (log_level_t)raw_level;
1062 
1063   uint8_t raw_direction = packet->direction;
1064   if (raw_direction > REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_CLIENT_TO_SERVER) {
1065     raw_direction = REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_UNKNOWN;
1066   }
1067   *out_direction = (remote_log_direction_t)raw_direction;
1068 
1069   uint16_t raw_flags = NET_TO_HOST_U16(packet->flags);
1070   *out_flags = raw_flags;
1071 
1072   uint32_t raw_message_length = NET_TO_HOST_U32(packet->message_length);
1073   if (raw_message_length > MAX_REMOTE_LOG_MESSAGE_LENGTH) {
1074     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Remote log message length too large: %u", raw_message_length);
1075   }
1076 
1077   size_t total_required = sizeof(remote_log_packet_t) + (size_t)raw_message_length;
1078   if (total_required > len) {
1079     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Remote log packet truncated: expected %zu bytes, have %zu",
1080                      total_required, len);
1081   }
1082 
1083   const uint8_t *message_bytes = (const uint8_t *)data + sizeof(remote_log_packet_t);
1084   size_t copy_len = raw_message_length;
1085   if (copy_len >= message_buffer_size) {
1086     copy_len = message_buffer_size - 1;
1087   }
1088 
1089   if (copy_len > 0) {
1090     memcpy(message_buffer, message_bytes, copy_len);
1091   }
1092   message_buffer[copy_len] = '\0';
1093 
1094   if (out_message_length) {
1095     *out_message_length = raw_message_length;
1096   }
1097 
1098   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1099 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    remote_log_packet_t::direction, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, remote_log_packet_t::flags,
    LOG_FATAL, remote_log_packet_t::log_level,
    MAX_REMOTE_LOG_MESSAGE_LENGTH,
    remote_log_packet_t::message_length, NET_TO_HOST_U16,
    NET_TO_HOST_U32, REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_CLIENT_TO_SERVER,
    REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_UNKNOWN, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by handle_remote_log_packet_from_client().






asciichat_error_t packet_send_error (socket_t
  sockfd, const crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx, asciichat_error_t
  error_code, const char * message)


Send an error packet with optional encryption context.


Parameters


sockfd Socket file descriptor


crypto_ctx Crypto context for encryption (NULL or not ready sends
  plaintext)


error_code Error code from
  asciichat_error_t enumeration


message Human-readable message to accompany the error (can be NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


When the crypto context is ready, the packet is encrypted
    automatically. During the handshake (or when encryption is disabled), the
    packet is sent in plaintext so protocol errors can be delivered before
    encryption is active.


Definition at line 898 of file packet.c.


899                                                          {
900   if (sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
901     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid socket descriptor");
902   }
903 
904   if (!message) {
905     message = "";
906   }
907 
908   size_t message_len = strnlen(message, MAX_ERROR_MESSAGE_LENGTH);
909   if (message_len == MAX_ERROR_MESSAGE_LENGTH) {
910     log_warn("Error message truncated to %zu bytes", (size_t)MAX_ERROR_MESSAGE_LENGTH);
911   }
912 
913   size_t payload_len = sizeof(error_packet_t) + message_len;
914   uint8_t *payload = SAFE_MALLOC(payload_len, uint8_t *);
915   if (!payload) {
916     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate %zu bytes for error packet", payload_len);
917   }
918 
919   error_packet_t *packet = (error_packet_t *)payload;
920   packet->error_code = HOST_TO_NET_U32((uint32_t)error_code);
921   packet->message_length = HOST_TO_NET_U32((uint32_t)message_len);
922 
923   if (message_len > 0) {
924     memcpy(payload + sizeof(error_packet_t), message, message_len);
925   }
926 
927   bool encryption_ready = crypto_ctx && crypto_is_ready(crypto_ctx);
928   asciichat_error_t send_result;
929 
930   if (encryption_ready) {
931     send_result =
932         send_packet_secure(sockfd, PACKET_TYPE_ERROR_MESSAGE, payload, payload_len, (crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx);
933   } else {
934     send_result = packet_send(sockfd, PACKET_TYPE_ERROR_MESSAGE, payload, payload_len);
935   }
936   SAFE_FREE(payload);
937 
938   if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
939     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send error packet: %s", asciichat_error_string(send_result));
940   }
941 
942   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
943 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, crypto_is_ready(),
    error_code, error_packet_t::error_code,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_NETWORK,
    HOST_TO_NET_U32, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, log_warn,
    MAX_ERROR_MESSAGE_LENGTH, error_packet_t::message_length,
    packet_send(), PACKET_TYPE_ERROR_MESSAGE, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, send_packet_secure(), and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by disconnect_client_for_bad_data().






asciichat_error_t packet_send_remote_log (socket_t
  sockfd, const crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx, log_level_t level,
  remote_log_direction_t direction, uint16_t flags, const char *
  message)


Send a remote log packet with optional encryption context.


Parameters


sockfd Socket file descriptor


crypto_ctx Crypto context for encryption (NULL or not ready sends
  plaintext)


level Log level to transmit


direction Direction flag (REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_*)


flags Additional flags (REMOTE_LOG_FLAG_*)


message Log message text


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 987 of file packet.c.


988                                                                                                                 {
989   if (sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
990     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid socket descriptor");
991   }
992 
993   if (level < LOG_DEV || level > LOG_FATAL) {
994     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid log level: %d", level);
995   }
996 
997   const char *safe_message = message ? message : "";
998   bool truncated = false;
999   size_t message_len = strnlen(safe_message, MAX_REMOTE_LOG_MESSAGE_LENGTH);
1000   if (message_len == MAX_REMOTE_LOG_MESSAGE_LENGTH && safe_message[message_len] != '\0') {
1001     truncated = true;
1002   }
1003 
1004   size_t payload_len = sizeof(remote_log_packet_t) + message_len;
1005   uint8_t *payload = SAFE_MALLOC(payload_len, uint8_t *);
1006   if (!payload) {
1007     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate %zu bytes for remote log packet", payload_len);
1008   }
1009 
1010   remote_log_packet_t *packet = (remote_log_packet_t *)payload;
1011   packet->log_level = (uint8_t)level;
1012   packet->direction = (uint8_t)direction;
1013   uint16_t final_flags = flags;
1014   if (truncated) {
1015     final_flags |= REMOTE_LOG_FLAG_TRUNCATED;
1016   }
1017   packet->flags = HOST_TO_NET_U16(final_flags);
1018   packet->message_length = HOST_TO_NET_U32((uint32_t)message_len);
1019 
1020   if (message_len > 0) {
1021     memcpy(payload + sizeof(remote_log_packet_t), safe_message, message_len);
1022   }
1023 
1024   bool encryption_ready = crypto_ctx && crypto_is_ready(crypto_ctx);
1025   asciichat_error_t send_result;
1026 
1027   if (encryption_ready) {
1028     int secure_result =
1029         send_packet_secure(sockfd, PACKET_TYPE_REMOTE_LOG, payload, payload_len, (crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx);
1030     send_result = secure_result == 0 ? ASCIICHAT_OK : ERROR_NETWORK;
1031   } else {
1032     send_result = packet_send(sockfd, PACKET_TYPE_REMOTE_LOG, payload, payload_len);
1033   }
1034 
1035   SAFE_FREE(payload);
1036 
1037   if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1038     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send remote log packet: %d", send_result);
1039   }
1040 
1041   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1042 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, crypto_is_ready(),
    remote_log_packet_t::direction, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_NETWORK,
    remote_log_packet_t::flags, HOST_TO_NET_U16,
    HOST_TO_NET_U32, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, LOG_FATAL,
    remote_log_packet_t::log_level, MAX_REMOTE_LOG_MESSAGE_LENGTH,
    remote_log_packet_t::message_length, packet_send(),
    PACKET_TYPE_REMOTE_LOG, REMOTE_LOG_FLAG_TRUNCATED,
    SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, send_packet_secure(), and
    SET_ERRNO.
